page: Amazon Web Services c8id.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 dedicated physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB.

Local storage is provided via a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network performance features a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-threaded performance, achieving top-tier ratings in single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.49 GB/sec and text processing at 2975.0 points. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally align with mid-range averages, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8487.0 and uncached memory read speeds of 79.76 GB/sec.

The c8id.2xlarge offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for compute-heavy workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space without network-attached storage overhead. The dedicated physical cores and high-speed DDR5 memory make it suitable for compute-intensive tasks, single-threaded data processing, software compilation, and moderate database operations. However, the 16 GB memory capacity represents a resource-density tradeoff for memory-bound applications, which may require scaling to memory-optimized instances.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 dedicated physical cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD local instance store. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers strong single-threaded performance, highlighted by top-tier results in bzip3 decompression and text processing benchmarks. While its multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics align with mid-range averages, the inclusion of Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets enhances its capability for specialized vector and matrix calculations. The c8id.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective for compute-heavy workloads requiring high-speed local storage, such as software compilation, data compression, and web serving, though its 2.0 GB per core memory ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-threaded performance for compute-heavy workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores, high-speed DDR5 memory, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs (4 physical cores) Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 474 GB NVMe SSD local instance store volume
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and text processing benchmarks
Support for Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
Best suited for compute-intensive tasks, software compilation, and web serving
